issues.claim: Know as imperishable that by which all this is pervaded; nobody can destroy this unchanging thing.
issues.objection: avyaya is rendered 'unchanging thing' — a register-faithful translation but slightly thin; the technical sense is 'without modification, undergoing none of the six vikāras.' The summary_paraphrase in the source pack carries the technical sense; the engineering layer's compression is acceptable for the register but loses the systematic 'no birth, no growth, no decay' structure. Acceptable as analog vocabulary.

issues.claim: Tatam, pervaded — the protocol lives everywhere in the system.
issues.objection: The Sanskrit tatam is preserved verbatim. The gloss 'lives everywhere in the system' is accurate as register translation of 'pervades.' Some pedants might prefer 'spread out' (the more literal sense), but 'pervades / lives everywhere' is widely used in Gita translations and is doctrinally faithful.

notes: The engineering layer preserves the three Sanskrit terms that carry doctrinal weight (avināśi, avyaya, tatam) verbatim with explanatory glosses — this is exactly the right move for register translation. The avyaya/tatam/avināśi triple matches the verse's grammar. The Advaita/Vishishtadvaita scope question (Brahman pervading universe vs. jīva pervading body) is correctly NOT taken up by the analog; the engineering layer stays inside cross-tradition consensus. The 'protocol pervades implementation' analogy is a register translation of 'sat pervades insentient matter' — defensible at this register. PASS.
